user_query:
I'm planning a week of short, student-friendly social posts to reinforce our biology lessons. Please use only the provided local files to create the content and a brief status summary, and include a simple validator I can run to check everything.

Inputs (do not use the Internet):
- input/lesson_outline.md
- input/misconceptions.csv
- input/calendar.csv

Deliverables:
1) out/posts.csv
   - Columns (in this order): date,topic_id,post_text,hashtags,reading_grade_estimate,target_audience
   - Requirements:
     - One and only one row for each date listed in input/calendar.csv (exact date string match).
     - topic_id must match a TopicID defined in input/lesson_outline.md.
     - post_text must be 280 characters or fewer, use student-friendly language, and include at least one emoji from this allowed set: 🔬🧪🧬🌿✨📚👍🤔.
     - hashtags must be a comma-separated list including both #BioClass and the SuggestedHashtag corresponding to the topic_id from input/lesson_outline.md (you may add additional hashtags, but at least two total must be present).
     - reading_grade_estimate must be an integer between 6 and 10 (inclusive).
     - target_audience must be either "students" or "parents".
     - Do not include links or references to external sources.

2) out/summary_report.md
   - A short status summary for our department newsletter with these sections (use these exact headings):
     - Plan Overview
     - Alignment
     - Misconceptions addressed
     - Schedule
   - Under "Misconceptions addressed", include a bullet list where each line follows exactly the pattern: "- TOPIC_ID: short note", covering every topic_id used in out/posts.csv and drawing the short note from input/misconceptions.csv.

3) validate.py
   - A Python script that performs all of the following checks and prints a human-readable summary to stdout:
     - Reads input/calendar.csv and verifies there is exactly one row in out/posts.csv for each date and no extra dates.
     - Extracts TopicIDs from input/lesson_outline.md by scanning lines that begin with "TopicID:" and verifies every topic_id in out/posts.csv is one of these.
     - For each post_text, verifies character length <= 280 and that it contains at least one emoji from the allowed set (🔬🧪🧬🌿✨📚👍🤔).
     - Verifies the hashtags field contains #BioClass and the SuggestedHashtag for the matching topic_id (the SuggestedHashtag should be read from input/lesson_outline.md by scanning lines that begin with "SuggestedHashtag:").
     - Verifies reading_grade_estimate is an integer between 6 and 10 (inclusive).
     - Verifies target_audience is either "students" or "parents".
     - Verifies out/summary_report.md contains the four required section headings and that under the "Misconceptions addressed" section there is a bullet line for every topic_id used in out/posts.csv, following exactly the pattern "- TOPIC_ID: ...".
   - Exit with code 0 if all checks pass; otherwise exit non-zero.

4) out/validation_log.txt
   - Run the validator with: python validate.py
   - Save the exact stdout to out/validation_log.txt.

Notes:
- Keep the tone friendly and accurate for high school learners (roughly 7th–9th grade reading level), and tie each post to the lesson outline. Make sure every deliverable path and format matches the above so I can reuse this with new weeks later. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
